name: Pecan-Chocolate Chip Pie
recipeInstructions:
FOR DOUGH: Cream the butter, cream cheese, and vanilla extract in a mixer or processor. Reduce speed to low and add the flour. Mix until just incorporated. Remove the dough, form into three balls, wrap in plastic wrap, and chill for one hour before using. Uncooked dough can be frozen up to two months. Freeze uncooked balls of dough or freeze dough in pie tins. Makes three 10-inch pies.
FOR FILLING: In a small saucepan over medium heat, mix the pecans and butter until the butter turns a golden color. Remove from heat and set aside. In a mixing bowl, combine the eggs, corn syrup, molasses, brown sugar, and vanilla extract. Mix until smooth, then mix in the pecans and butter. Pour this filling into the pie crusts and sprinkle with the chocolate chips. Place on the middle rack of a preheated 350°F oven and bake until the center of the pie is set, about 35 to 40 minutes. Turn off the heat and let the pie cool before serving.
